meaning: n.鐐稿脊,绐佸彂鑰屾儕浜虹殑浜嬬墿
examples: His resignation is a political bombshell.
The news of his death came as a bombshell.
She dropped a bombshell at the meeting and announced that she was leaving.
For more on stress tests, see why Fed tests reveal a European bank bombshell.
The news about Celebrex was not the only bombshell dropped on the drug industry today.
